hero.title: I build interfaces
hero.subtitle: that people love to use
hero.description: I’m a frontend developer with 6+ years in product teams. I ship clear, modern UIs with strong product sense, and I love turning ideas into user-friendly features. I can help with light Node and serverless work, but the frontend is what I do best. Open to both freelance and in-house opportunities.

about.title: Where
about.subtitle: About me
about.description: I’m Mariandrea, a frontend developer. I’m not a full-time web designer, and I wouldn’t compare my visual craft to a dedicated UI specialist—but in every role I’ve been the one who ends up leading interface work when there isn’t a separate designer, and teams have always been very happy with the result. I care about how things look and how they work.
about.subdescription: I’m at my best shipping features in a modern, accessible stack, with solid judgment on product and UX. I also write Node and serverless when the product needs it, but I don’t manage databases—frontend is the work I want to do every day. My background is 100% in-house: I don’t have a public freelance project list, because I’ve always worked in companies, not as a solo contractor, and I don’t have a trophy shelf—just consistent delivery in real products.

scrollyCase.title: From a legacy marketing site to a
scrollyCase.subtitle: MedBioinformatics: hierarchy, messaging, and components brought in line. Scroll inside the frame for the full capture; as you move down the page, before and after blend.
scrollyCase.subtitleReduced: MedBioinformatics: same comparison in two frames. Scroll inside each one to see the full capture.
scrollyCase.step1Body: Dense information and a less guided first read—everything competing for attention at once.
scrollyCase.step2Body: Typography, whitespace, and repeatable modules that match the product story.
scrollyCase.step3Body: A researcher-first narrative and trust in the data, closer to a best-in-class SaaS presence.
scrollyCase.scrollHint: Keep scrolling the page to animate the before / after blend.
scrollyCase.frameHint: Scroll inside the frame to move through the full capture.

scrollyDisgenet.title: From a data-heavy product to a
scrollyDisgenet.subtitle: Four full-page captures: two from the old flow (home and a deeper screen) and two from the new approach. Scroll inside the frame to see each page; as you move down, the view crossfades through all four in order.
scrollyDisgenet.subtitleReduced: The same four-screen journey in a grid: four frames, each with a scrollable full capture.
scrollyDisgenet.step1Body: A dense, table-led surface—what the product is for is slow to read.
scrollyDisgenet.step2Body: A second screen from the classic flow: the story is there, but it doesn’t read fast.
scrollyDisgenet.step3Title: The turn: same complexity, new clarity
scrollyDisgenet.step3Body: The redesign leans on hierarchy, space, and blocks so researchers and clinicians can scan with confidence.
scrollyDisgenet.step4Body: First screen of the new line: value, CTAs, and a clearer scientific product signal.
scrollyDisgenet.step5Body: Second new screen: search, metrics, or an inner flow aligned with the new visual system.
scrollyDisgenet.scrollHint: Scroll the page: the frame crossfades through all four phases.
scrollyDisgenet.frameHint: Scroll inside the frame to read each full-height capture.
scrollyDisgenet.reducedHint: No page-scroll blend; a fixed four-frame grid (reduced motion).

scrollyMaia.ariaLabel: Case study: biomedical AI assistant redesign
scrollyMaia.title: From a single chat to a
scrollyMaia.subtitle: Evolution from MAIA to AIDA (AI DISGENET Assistant): dual panes (chat and terminal), visible session tools, and a single question bar. No public link—this is a design comparison only.
scrollyMaia.subtitleReduced: Same before/after in two frames. Scroll each window to see the full capture.
scrollyMaia.step1Body: Two columns, but the product story sits behind a simple chat: execution feels secondary to the text box.
scrollyMaia.step2Body: Identity, controls, and status grouped so it’s clear where to type, which model is active, and where agent output lands.
scrollyMaia.step3Body: A console with two clear modules (conversation and execution traces), session actions, and one path to the input—closer to a real scientific product.
scrollyMaia.beforeAlt: MAIA: previous screen with chat, terminal, and header actions
scrollyMaia.afterAlt: AIDA: proposal with dual terminals, header, and input bar
scrollyMaia.scrollHint: Scroll the page to crossfade the old interface into the new one.
scrollyMaia.frameHint: Scroll inside the frame to move through the full capture.

items.title: Frontend development
items.description: React, TypeScript, modern tools—clean, maintainable UIs in production codebases.

items.title: Product & features
items.description: I enjoy discovering how to turn product goals into shippable, user-friendly features.

items.title: UI & strong UX
items.description: I’m a developer first, but I own interface quality when there’s no designer in the room.

items.title: Design systems
items.description: Reusable components, shared patterns, and consistent UI for growing products.

items.title: Node & serverless
items.description: Lightweight endpoints and serverless when the product needs it—not deep database or infra work; frontend stays my main lane.

items.title: Performance & quality
items.description: Practical web performance, accessibility awareness, and maintainable code.

contact.description: If the fit is there—project, long-term, or in-house—I’m easy to reach. A few lines of context is enough; we take it from there.
